title: helianthemum umbellatum var. subdecumbens
intro: Helianthemum umbellatum var. subdecumbens, also known as Rockrose or Frostweed, is a low-growing, woody perennial plant in the family Cistaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming Rockrose is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and even acting as an antiviral agent. Rockrose can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used in folk medicine to alleviate colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
